title: Browsing ProcScript Modules in the Uniface Debugger
breadcrumbs: Browsing ProcScript Modules in the Uniface Debugger
text: Browsing ProcScript Modules in the Uniface Debugger Navigate to specific ProcScript modules in components or global ProcScript using the Debugger Browser, and use the browser to set breakpoints and watches. From the Debugger main window, choose View > Browser . The browser window is displayed. Tip: From the Call Stack window, you can open the browser at a specific module by double-clicking it. Double-click the appropriate icon to expand its structure. If ProcScript code is available, the code is displayed in the Trigger/Module pane. To open another structure, click the open icon in the browser's toolbar, and select a structure. Choose a structure of Type . If you chose Global ProcScript in the Type field, choose the source from the Look in field. Optionally specify a Subsytem and Structure Name or a Library . You can enter a name, a retrieve profile, or leave it blank (the same as GOLD *). Click Retrieve to retrieve all structures matching your retrieve profiles. Then click Open . To toggle in a displayed ProcScript module, select the ProcScript line in the Trigger/Module pane and click the breakpoints icon in the browser's toolbar. To watch a field or variable, highlight the field or variable name in the Structure pane, right-click and choose Add Watch . To set or remove a break point, right-click the line of code and choose Toggle Break Point . To edit the value of a field or variable, double-click the field or variable name. Alternatively, highlight the field or variable name in the Structure pane, right-click, and choose a value. The Edit Field, Variable, or Watch form is displayed. Related Links Open Structure Select a structure (a component or global ProcScript) to be opened for the Debugger Browser window. The Open Structure form is accessible only via the open icon in the browser’s toolbar. Monitoring ProcScript Modules The Uniface Debugger allows you to monitor and control the execution of ProcScript. Call Stack View the ProcScript call stack to see the modules that are called or activated at runtime, or to identify where to put a try-catch block to handle exceptions. Was this topic helpful?
